title: benzoin subumbelliflorum
intro: Benzoin subumbelliflorum, also known as Styrax tonkinensis, is a plant species in the family Styracaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The resin extracted from this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic effects. The health benefits of benzoin subumbelliflorum include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of benzoin subumbelliflorum can be found in the form of resin, oil, or tincture, which are often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and other health problems.
